title: Contact
text:
Yutaka Sumino
Department of Applied Physics, Tokyo University of Science
Currently on sabbatical at the University of Surrey
Email: ysumino[at]rs.tus.ac.jp

title: Yutaka Sumino
text: I study active motion of mesoscopic interfaces such as droplets, vesicles, and colloids driven by chemical reactions and interfacial flows.

title: Research
text:
I am interested in establishing a physical framework for life-like systems that exhibit autonomous motion, collective dynamics, and emergent structures.
This direction aims to connect experimental model systems with coarse-grained theories for non-equilibrium dynamics.

title: Yutaka Sumino
text:
Active Turbulence in Biological Systems

My work explores active flows and pattern formation in biological and biomimetic systems, including cytoskeletal active matter and confined geometries.

title: Yutaka Sumino
text:
Reduced Models and Stochastic Dynamics
I develop reduced mathematical descriptions, including effective stochastic equations, to extract essential dynamical principles from complex systems.

title: Yutaka Sumino
text:
Understanding how life-like behaviors emerge from physical systems.
Artificial Life Physics seeks universal physical principles behind self-organization, pattern formation, and collective dynamics.
Rather than focusing only on molecular components, we investigate general mechanisms underlying emergent dynamics.
Our research focuses on mesoscopic interfaces (100 nm – 1 mm), where transport, mechanics, and geometry interact to generate rich nonequilibrium phenomena.
My recent work aims to bridge active biological systems and non-biological systems through nonlinear dynamics and stochastic coarse-grained models.









Artificial Life Physics
Mesoscopic interfaces with active flows
Active turbulence in biological and synthetic systems
Nonlinear dynamics and pattern formation in active matter
Reduced models and Langevin descriptions
